description: Behavioural obligations for the ack (¬ß14) + presence (¬ß15) primitives ‚Äî semantics a JSON Schema cannot check; proven against a conformant Hub + its consuming agent (exercised by the reference ack.test.ts / presence.test.ts).
obligation: ACK (¬ß14): (1) Terminal-once ‚Äî the FIRST ack for an `in_reply_to` wins and is immutable; a repeat returns the existing ack. (2) Submitter-bound ‚Äî only the submitting agent may `POST /v1/messages/{id}/ack` its response; a foreign/unknown id is `not_found`; acking an `open` (non-terminal) message is refused. (3) Directive receipt folds into the mailbox consume ‚Äî `POST /v1/inbox/ack` with an optional `note` both consumes and acknowledges. (4) Delivery track ‚Äî orthogonal to the ¬ß7 `resolution`: directive queued‚Üídelivered(drained)‚Üíacknowledged; ask/task answered‚Üídelivered-to-agent(push 2xx or the agent's authenticated GET)‚Üíacknowledged. PRESENCE (¬ß15): (5) `last_seen` is derived from the agent's authenticated poll/long-poll/SSE activity (no heartbeat); state is `online` within the freshness window, `offline` when stale, `unknown` when never seen. (6) Owner-only ‚Äî only the human whose account the agent belongs to may read presence; a non-owned/unknown agent reads as `unknown`, never leaking activity timing.
